transcript.text: Hello, living in Lafayette V-LOG. It's like 1130 at night on the 28th of April, 2013.
So I managed to get it under the wire and not miss a day, because that's the idea.
So let's see, yesterday I did Strava for the first time. I went out and pushed start on the bike computer.
That's the Garmin 510.
Somebody wanted me to do a video about it, but I can't really do a video about that because it's just so simple.
All I do is, I mean, I can do a video of it. This is what it looks like.
It's $400. I got it for like $340, though, because I got the, you know, the $60 dividend.
So I went into REI and got that.
And then for the running, I do this, and I got this for my kayak trip, and it works on it, too.
It's the Garmin 400, 205.
So, yeah, so yesterday I just pushed start, and then I rode out to the road, and then I rode back,
Because there's a section right here that somebody set up.
I guess you set up a section. Maybe I'll talk about Strava later, but then I just plugged it in,
And, yeah, it just automatically does it.
So I didn't even know how to work these things.
All I have to do is push start and then plug it in.
It does everything itself.
It tells you, you know, how far you went, how fast you averaged, how many feet, the elevation gain you did,
And what place I got. I got a 162nd place out of, like, 320 people or something like that.
And then I tried it with the watch today, where I went on and I had this other section that's right out there.
That's the great thing about living in, like, this front range is everybody does this.
So there's sections and millions of runners all over the place.
So I got fourth place over that, which was last place.
But it works.
So same thing with the watch.
You just push start and stop when you're done, and then you plug it up, and it does everything.
I mean, like, look, oh, it's because then today I did Green Mountain and Boulder.
So I got 32nd place, which is second to last, up Green Mountain or whatever.
But there's all these sections, too, little mini sections, and I was getting, like, second in some of them and stuff.
So, yeah, that's what I did yesterday.
And yesterday, what else did I do?
Spend the time doing, figuring that out.
And then, yeah, and today, yeah, alarm clock went off at 6.
So I slept in until, like, 7.15, though, because it was weird.
Even though I only ran, like, a mile yesterday, I rode a mile yesterday.
And then I think I ran, like, a mile or something.
I felt like I had rode 100 miles, dude.
I, like, was exhausted from that.
You know when you're all feverish and tired and everything?
So, yeah, I'm in bad shape.
Soon today, I got a little bit, like, ugh.
I figured out this thing.
Oh, yeah, I went on the run today because I figured that out.
And then my brother came over.
He's in town and got a mountain bike off my roommate and was hanging out with him for a little bit.
And then I went into town and went to my mom's house, went in the basement, and went through some stuff and grabbed some things.
And then went to the running store and got some shorts and anti-chafe cream.
And then went over and ran up to the top of Green Mountain in Boulder.
I was going to film it, but you can't do Zoom in my cell phone.
I didn't want to do it in my cell phone.
And I couldn't do my video camera because I left my card in my computer.
So I'll film Green Mountain next time I do it.
But, yeah, I just put start and I went up.
But it's like a – I don't know exactly how long it is.
But it's, you know, it's just like, you know, the town and you go to the top of the hill and then back down to be like an hour or whatever.
But, like, yeah, dude, I was in bad shape.
Like, I was running for a little bit at the beginning.
And then, like, my legs kind of, like, stopped working.
Like, I couldn't move my legs.
And then I had to, like, lean against trees and stuff.
People were, like, walking by me.
And I thought I wasn't going to be able to make it, but I made it.
And then I was running down and I, you know, I fell and slipped on the ice, you know, hurt myself and stuff.
And, yeah, and then, like, and then I bonked.
And it was just weird because I thought I had, like, had food before I went.
But, yeah, next time I have to bring, like, power bars and stuff.
And at one point I was walking down and there was this girl going up.
She stopped.
She literally looked at me like this.
And I was like, what is she looking like that for?
And I was like, oh, yeah, it must be because I'm staggering.
I must have been, like, totally, like, staggering.
And then I went back to my car.
And, yeah, and then, but then my mom, like, took us all out to a movie because my brother's in town.
And I was bonked, but I got a Slurpee.
Those are good.
And, like, a bunch of, like, runts.
And that brought me back to life in the movie.
And we watched this movie, Place Beyond the Pines, with Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper.
And I was, like, bored out of my mind for, like, the first half of it or the second act or whatever.
But then, you know, get attached to the characters.
And we want to know what happens in the end.
So I kind of gained interest.
I don't know why my mom chose that movie.
I don't like the genre of movie, like, with, like, just where sad stuff happens.
But, anyways, and then I got some beers and had a couple beers, or one beer with my family down there.
And, yeah, so now it's, like, 1130, you know, because I just plugged that in.
So I think I'm going to sleep in tomorrow.
But that was, like, my first, like, day of actual exercise.
So, I mean, I managed to do the mountains, so I'm coming forward.
I listen to music, though, so I think I'm going to start listening to, like, audiobooks or whatever.
Tomorrow I'm going to figure out how to put the audiobooks back on the Kindle.
I had all of my audiobooks, you know, like 750 of them.
You can see my website on my Kindle, but then the Kindle, like, deletes them all the time.
It's just fucked.
Like, the B-book was way better.
I wish I had a B-book, because you put it on the B-book and it stays there.
But the Kindle, and I hope they don't change the fucking rules and shit where you can't get them for free anymore.
The ones that you could before that I have on my website, because I spent so much time, like, what I put on my website where all the books you can get for free.
But I think it was pretty much, they should be, because it's, like, the rules are, you know, if it's before 1923.
But that's what I'm going to do tomorrow, figure that out, and figure out a way to put it on the Kabar.
I haven't ridden on my rollers yet while I'm working on the computer.
I get in the habit of doing that.
And, yeah, so that's about, I guess, all I have to say.
Peace out.
